task: Verify the core blind-debate experience through the real browser UI.
notes: This file is the browser-enforced subset of the broader LSD v1.2 design criteria.
It focuses on requirements that are already represented in the current app surface and can be verified end to end through the UI.

criteria.title: Create a debate from the UI from a blank-start snapshot state
checks: When the app starts without an active debate, the create-debate form is visible.
Submitting a motion, moderation criteria, and debate frame creates the active debate and unlocks posting.
The public posting surface remains identity-blind and centered on arguments rather than contributors.

criteria.title: Submit opposing argument units from the UI
checks: A FOR post can be submitted from the browser.
An AGAINST post can be submitted from the browser.
The pending-post count and list reflect both submissions.

criteria.title: Generate an immutable scoring snapshot from the UI
checks: Generating a snapshot from the browser succeeds.
Pending posts reset after the snapshot completes.
Snapshot metadata and verdict data appear in the page header.

criteria.title: Inspect topic and verdict decision surfaces through the UI
checks: The Topics page renders at least one topic row after a snapshot.
The Verdict page renders overall scores and topic contributions after a snapshot.

criteria.title: Persist moderation template changes through the Admin UI
checks: An authenticated user can save a moderation template draft from admin.html.
Reloading admin.html preserves the saved draft in template history.

criteria.title: Render evidence, dossier, and governance trust surfaces from live APIs
checks: Evidence page loads summary content from /api/debate/evidence-targets.
Decision dossier page renders snapshot-linked diagnostics from /api/debate/decision-dossier.
Governance page renders frame and health summary data from governance endpoints.

criteria.title: Complete register, login, and logout flows through UI forms
checks: register.html creates an account and persists token plus user metadata in localStorage.
login.html authenticates the same account and respects the next= redirect target.
Logout clears local auth/session state and redirects back to login with user feedback.

criteria.title: Render snapshot history and latest snapshot diff after multiple runs
checks: snapshot.html history table shows at least two rows after two snapshots are generated.
Latest diff view renders old/new snapshot ids and summary bullets from /api/debate/snapshot-diff.
History and diff surfaces stay available through the normal UI (not raw JSON).

criteria.title: Debate proposal lifecycle: submit, queue, accept
checks: A regular user can submit a debate proposal via /api/debate-proposals.
The proposal appears in the admin review queue and the user's own proposals list.
An admin can accept the proposal, which creates a debate and updates the proposal status.

criteria.title: Identity-blind public surface hardening
checks: Authenticated nav shows a generic 'Account' indicator instead of display_name or email.
No personal identity strings appear in the public debate surfaces.

criteria.title: Snapshot integrity and reproducibility fields
checks: snapshot.html renders input_hash_root and output_hash_root.
Recipe versions are visible for component traceability.
Replay manifest is available in a collapsible detail view.
